spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates the feature's general idea but doesn't surface the named spec API symbols directly.

interactive.rationale: Real controls + script-driven side-effects make this hands-on, not a static card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Most of the blurb's promises land but a few of the named behaviours aren't shown.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is covered but only a few failure modes are surfaced.

references.rationale: Cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> reference link as required.

design_system.rationale: Has 15 raw hex colour value(s) inline — should use --bg-*/--text-*/--accent-* tokens instead.

openQuestions.title: Surface more named spec API for gamepad-event-driven-input-api
openQuestions.detail: The spec summary lists API surfaces this concept doesn't currently name on-page. Either rename the controls / readouts to mention them, or add a second concept page that targets the missing surface directly. The reviewer extracted these from the spec: Gamepad event-driven input API. New events: gamepadbuttondown / gamepadbuttonup / gamepadbuttonchange / gamepadaxismove on Window, in addition to existing gamepadconnected / gamepaddisconnected. GamepadButtonEvent / GamepadAxisEvent expose gamepad index, button index, value. Removes need to poll navigator.getGamepads() in rAF. Edge cases: deadzone handling, mapping (standard vs non-standard layout...

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one failure mode
openQuestions.detail: The page currently shows the happy path. Add a UI affordance that drives the feature into a failure mode the spec explicitly calls out (timeout, denied permission, cross-origin block, malformed input) and surface what the browser actually does.

openQuestions.title: Tighten the design-system compliance
openQuestions.detail: Either replace raw hex with --bg-*/--text-*/--accent-* tokens, or pair every button background override with a color override (the global button rule sets both — overriding one alone risks light-on-light).

openQuestions.title: Reconcile blurb and page contents
openQuestions.detail: The feature index blurb promises behaviours that the page doesn't yet exercise. Either trim the blurb to match what the page does, or add the missing UI.

summary: Concept needs follow-up work before it really lands. 4 open question(s) queued.
